source.edition_note: Canonical Preamble record from the Sringeri witness

text.source_text: युष्मदस्मत्प्रत्ययगोचरयो र्विषयविषयिणो स्तमःप्रकाशवद्विरुद्धस्वभावयो रितरेतर भावानुपपत्तौ सिद्धायाम् , तद्धर्माणामपि सुतरा मि तरेतरभावानुपपत्तिः — इत्यतः अस्मत्प्रत्ययगोचरे विषयिणि चिदात्मके युष्म त्प्रत्ययगोचरस्य विषयस्य तद्धर्माणां चा ध्यासः तद्विपर्ययेण विषयिणस्तद्धर्माणां च विषयेऽध्यासो मि थ्येति भवितुं युक्तम् । तथा प्य न्योन्यस्मि न्नन्योन्यात्मकता मन्योन्यधर्मांश्चा ध्य स्येतरेतराविवेकेन अत्यन्त विविक्तयो र्धर्मधर्मिणोः मिथ्या ज्ञाननिमित्तः सत्या नृते मिथुनीकृत्य ‘ अह मिदम् ’ ‘ ममेदम् ’ इति नैसर्गिको ऽयं लोक व्यवहारः ॥
text.transliteration_iast: yuṣmadasmatpratyayagocarayo rviṣayaviṣayiṇo stamaḥprakāśavadviruddhasvabhāvayo ritaretara bhāvānupapattau siddhāyām , taddharmāṇāmapi sutarā mi taretarabhāvānupapattiḥ — ityataḥ asmatpratyayagocare viṣayiṇi cidātmake yuṣma tpratyayagocarasya viṣayasya taddharmāṇāṃ cā dhyāsaḥ tadviparyayeṇa viṣayiṇastaddharmāṇāṃ ca viṣaye'dhyāso mi thyeti bhavituṃ yuktam | tathā pya nyonyasmi nnanyonyātmakatā manyonyadharmāṃścā dhya syetaretarāvivekena atyanta viviktayo rdharmadharmiṇoḥ mithyā jñānanimittaḥ satyā nṛte mithunīkṛtya ‘ aha midam ’ ‘ mamedam ’ iti naisargiko 'yaṃ loka vyavahāraḥ ||
text.segmentation_note: BS 1.1 preamble opening block (BS_C01_S01_I01) as extracted witness text.

translation.literal_translation: When the mutual non-coincidence of the subject and object, which fall within the sphere of the notions of you and I and are opposed in nature like darkness and light, has been established, the mutual non-coincidence of their properties also necessarily follows. Therefore, in the subject, the self that falls within the sphere of the I-notion, there is superimposition of the object that falls within the sphere of the you-notion, and of its properties; and conversely, upon the subject there is superimposition of the subject and its properties upon the object, which is false. Thus it is fitting to say that the ordinary world-process arises from a mistaken cognition that mixes together mutually distinct subject and object, the real and the unreal, and says: ‘I am this’ and ‘this is mine.’
translation.technical_translation: Once the impossibility of mutual identity between object and subject, as they are cognized within the fields of the you-notion and the I-notion and are contrary in nature like darkness and light, has been established, the impossibility of mutual identity among their properties is likewise established. Therefore, in the subject, the conscious self that is the field of the I-notion, there is adhyasa of the object, the field of the you-notion, together with its properties; and by reversal there is adhyasa of the subject together with its properties in the object. Since this is mithya, the ordinary व्यवहार of the world rightly consists in a natural misrecognition of what is utterly distinct as subject and object, in which the real and the unreal are conjoined and taken as ‘I’ and ‘mine.’
translation.interpretive_note: This is the opening definition of adhyasa and the doctrinal threshold of the whole commentary. The separate Sanskrit note for Padmapada's व्याख्या is preserved adjacent to this record.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Erroneous reciprocal attribution of self and non-self, and of their properties
Marks the false status of the superimposed world-process

uncertainty_notes: Canonical preamble translation created from the fresh restart baseline.
Padmapada's Sanskrit व्याख्या is preserved in a separate adjunct note.
